title: WhatsApp rolls out username feature; not all users are happy
description: On June 29, WhatsApp introduced a new feature that enables users to reserve usernames for themselves, similar to what Telegram, another messaging app, launched in 2014. The WhatsApp feature will be rolled out over the coming months, but users can reserve usernames of their choice from this week; they will be notified once the feature becomes available in their country. The app will provide a username generator to help users—there are 3 billion of globally—make a pick. The feature will help creators, small businesses and organisations, who need to maintain a consistent presence online, claim their existing Instagram or Facebook username on WhatsApp.The Meta-owned messaging app said in its blog post on June 29 that this feature is a step towards increased privacy for users: “Usernames are our latest step to make WhatsApp even more private. There's no directory to browse and no suggestions—people will need to know your exact username to contact you for the first time. To help control who can reach you on WhatsApp with your username, we've built an optional username key that others will need to know to message you.”Users can choose to enable the feature if they want to keep their phone numbers private. At present, anyone who has your phone number can contact you on WhatsApp, but if you have enabled the new feature, they will need to know your username to contact you and will not be able to see your phone number. If you have already saved the person’s phone number, you will continue to see the name under which you saved that number.Your username will appear across direct messages, group chats and calls, and will be displayed with an @ symbol to distinguish it from a display name or phone number. To make sharing easier, WhatsApp will allow users to generate a QR code or a shareable link that directs others to their username. The feature is available on mobile devices and not on WhatsApp Web or Desktop.According to WhatsApp, the username should be between three and 35 characters long, can contain lowercase letters, digits, and special characters like underscores and periods. It cannot be a string of numbers only, it must be unique and can’t contain restricted words or phrases. Users can create, change or delete their username at any time, however, there will be a limit to the number of changes; previous usernames will become available to others after 14 days. Usernames can be transferred to new phone numbers.Also Read: WhatsApp Lawsuit Explained: Privacy Claims, Message Access and India’s DPDP ActWhatsApp will also offer an optional username key, which, when enabled, will require the other person to use both the username and the key to contact you. Existing contacts, people who have already chatted with, members of shared groups, those who have scanned your QR code, or anyone the user has messaged first will not need the key. The key is generated by WhatsApp and can be changed, regenerated or removed at any time. Enabling the feature will not affect existing conversations.The username feature has been announced days after Kunal Shah was announced as the new global head of WhatsApp, as the company aims to monetise its presence in India, where it has 500 million users. The broader vision is to transform WhatsApp into a ‘super app’ offering a wider range of services.“Timing is everything. Joined WhatsApp early enough to claim my username before we release this to the world. Time to get yours. A more private way to connect. Coming soon to your WhatsApp,” wrote Shah on X.In response to Shah’s post, Telegram wrote on X, “October 2014, is that you?”, while adding a link to its blog post from 12 years ago when it had rolled out the same feature.Everyone, however, is not as excited about the new feature. Entrepreneur and author Ankur Warikoo raised a red flag, saying scammers and fraudsters can create usernames that appear familiar and deceive users. “In a country such as India, this could be a disaster, if the right anti-abuse systems are not set up by WhatsApp,” he wrote on X.Jasveer Singh, CEO of KnotDating, a matrimony site, wrote on X, “WhatsApp just launched usernames. My first thought wasn't privacy - it was scams”, while Vijay Shekhar Sharma, founder and CEO of Paytm, wrote, “Soon you will have verified username on WhatsApp, and then unverified similar-sounding usernames… which in turn will…”WhatsApp has not mentioned if and how the username feature will be integrated with WhatsApp Payments.

title: Innovative companies in India
description: India's dynamic business landscape is being transformed by a new generation of visionary enterprises that are redefining industry standards through innovation, strategic thinking, and purposeful growth. These forward-looking organizations are driving change across sectors by embracing cutting-edge technologies, sustainable practices, customer-centric approaches, and disruptive business models. Their commitment to excellence, adaptability, and long-term impact is not only strengthening India's economic ecosystem but also enhancing its global competitiveness. This curated recognition celebrates companies that continue to push boundaries, inspire progress, and contribute significantly to shaping the future of business and innovation in India.Sungrow India Pvt. Ltd.Sunil Badesra, Country DirectorSungrow, a global leader in renewable energy technology, has pioneered sustainable power solutions for over 29 years. As of Dec 2025, Sungrow has installed over 1000 GW of power electronic converters worldwide. The company is recognized as the world's most bankable PV inverter and energy storage company (BloombergNEF). Its innovations power clean energy projects across the globe, supported by a network of 520 service outlets guaranteeing excellent customer experiences. At Sungrow, we're committed to bridging to a sustainable future through cutting-edge technology and unparalleled service. Since its establishment in 1997, the Company has been concentrating on the field of new energy power generation, adhering to market demand orientation, and taking technological innovation as the propellant for development. The company has cultivated a professional R&D team with solid R&D experiences and strong capabilities for independent innovation.LocatR.ai Hemant Agarwal, FounderBuilding the Intelligence Layer for Modern LogisticsLocatR.ai is an AI-powered logistics intelligence platform transforming how businesses manage fleets, field operations, and last-mile delivery. Founded by Hemant Agarwal, the company combines transport management, workforce tracking, computer vision, IoT, and real-time analytics into a unified operating system that delivers complete visibility across assets, orders, and personnel.Built for India’s operating realities, LocatR.ai performs reliably even in low-bandwidth environments, enabling seamless execution across complex logistics networks. Its defining innovation is “ground-truth intelligence” - validating operational events through verifiable, data-backed evidence rather than simply tracking movement.Trusted by enterprises including Exide Industries, Meesho, Flipkart, ElasticRun, and LoadShare, the platform has helped reduce return-related losses by over 80% while cutting dispute resolution times from months to less than an hour. By turning operational data into actionable intelligence, LocatR.ai is helping build a more transparent, accountable, and efficient logistics ecosystem.MiBOT Ventures India Pvt. Ltd.Vivek Dilip, CEOMiBOT is building a hardware-agnostic embodied AI platform for real-time safety, surveillance, inspection, and compliance intelligence across high-risk enterprise environments like Construction, Oil & Gas, Power, Manufacturing, Airports & Ports.MiBOT's platform integrates seamlessly with existing CCTV infrastructure and Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs). MiBOT is the first company in the world to  to detect 100+ safety, operational, compliance, and surveillance anomalies in real time.Bizwin Solutions Pvt. Ltd.Aurijit Ganguli, Managing DirectorMost B2B companies have strong offerings and credible delivery. What holds them back is the sales system built around them.Bizwin transforms that system to deliver predictable revenue for its B2B clients across IT, Manufacturing, and Professional Services industries.What make Bizwin genuinely innovative are its proprietary SAGE framework and ADVICE methodology.  Leveraging these, Bizwin embeds senior practitioners — averaging 30+ years global sales experience— directly inside client sales teams. The result is a structured, self-sustaining revenue engine, not a consultant's report.The outcomes are real and measurable: 30–60% revenue growth within 12 months, proposal win rates improvement from 20% to 40% in 5 months, and many more.Equally interesting is Bizwin’s commercial model. It charges 1% success fee on incremental revenue generated – one of the few consulting companies in the world that stakes its fee on the client's growth.BuildingWorld.aiMukesh Dhirajlal & Daksha Mukesh, DirectorsIndia’s construction and interiors sector is worth more than ₹3 lakh crore annually, yet homeowners still struggle to bridge the gap between inspiration and execution. BuildingWorld.ai was created to solve that problem.Founded in 2022, the AI-powered visual commerce platform enables users to visualize spaces, identify materials from images, discover design ideas, source products, and connect with verified suppliers and professionals—all within a single ecosystem. Powered by six proprietary in-house AI tools trained on Indian design data, the platform is building digital infrastructure for the country’s-built environment.Ahead of launch, BuildingWorld.ai has aggregated 77,500+ products, 19,000+ curated design inspirations, 4,200+ brands, 83,600+ dealers, and 6,600+ professionals, creating one of India’s largest design-to-procurement networks.The company is jointly led by architect Mukesh Dhirajlal, Daksha Mukesh and Nikita Gandhi, combining decades of industry expertise with a shared vision to transform how India designs, sources, and builds. BurgrillShreh Madan, Co-founder & CMO Founded in 2016, Burgrill has grown into one of India’s leading homegrown QSR burger brands with 70+ outlets across the country. As the OGs of grilled burgers in India, we set out to prove that fast food can be both delicious and better for you.Long before protein became a mainstream conversation, Burgrill was building its menu around high-protein, wholesome ingredients. From grilled burgers and spinach tortilla wraps to an entire range of healthy bowls, we’ve consistently offered better alternatives without compromising on taste.Positioned in the mass-premium segment, Burgrill combines quality, affordability, and innovation to cater to the evolving Indian consumer palette. With a grilling-first vision, centralized quality control, and a commitment to mindful eating, Burgrill continues to redefine what modern QSR dining can look like in India.iOTA Infotech Pvt. Ltd.Rahul Layal,Founder & CEOCreativity was an afterthought in North India’s creative landscape, until the emergence of iOTA - A Creative Agency founded by Rahul Layal with the ethos that world-class work did not need a metropolitan address, it can be built from anywhere, even a Tier-2 City. iOTA has since evolved into a global creative company with 150+ members across the world, delivering genre-defining work across India, North America, Dubai and the Gulf region. The company invests heavily in R&D and emerging technology, weaving AI into its creative and growth workflows to stay ahead of where brands are headed next. The agency has worked with legacy brands and industry juggernauts like Reckitt, Tata 1mg, Konica Minolta, Pepsi, Wendy’s, XYXX, Milton, CK12, Muviq and YouTube to name a few. iOTA boasts a decorated portfolio of 40+ awards & titles, courtesy of its great work and its inclusive & progressive work-culture, which is reflected through their remarkable client & team retention and their widely beloved social media presence.AASOKAMonica Malhotra Kandhari, MDAASOKA is a comprehensive blended learning solution that provides a customised curriculum solution for K-12. AASOKA works with schools to provide them with the tools and services needed to upgrade both administrative and teaching-learning processes. The NEP 2020 and NCF-aligned model curriculum ensures interactive, engaging learning experiences for the 21st-century learners. Catering to CBSE, ICSE, and various state boards, AASOKA has partnered with 5,500+ schools, benefiting over 10,00,000 students and training 80,000+ teachers annually.AASOKA also offers AASOKA WizKids and AI STEAM Lab for students aged 4+ and 8+, respectively. WizKids builds foundational literacy, numeracy, and computational thinking through interactive activities and a screenless coding bot, while AI STEAM Lab promotes hands-on learning, creativity, and problem-solving across Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM).AASOKA was ranked no.1 in the World’s Top EdTech Rising Stars of 2025 by TIME and Statista.Mad Result  Abhishek Karnik, Founder  In 2019, when Abhishek Karnik started Mad Result from a small Mumbai setup, it was based on one simple belief of how marketing should be defined in terms of revenue rather than vanity matrix. That belief, seven years ago, has since turned into one of the fastest-growing performance marketing agencies in India.The numbers reveal the narrative of growth. Mad Result scaled from its initial clients to 200+ brands across real estate, BFSI, and e-commerce, managing ₹34 crore+ in ad spends, delivering 200 million+ impressions over 3,000+ campaigns with over ₹150 crore collective client revenue. Its journey from owning a steady 6x ROI to being acclaimed as one of the best performance marketing companies in Mumbai has been legendary."We grew by obsessing over outcomes clients could bank, not just clicks," says Abhishek Karnik, Founder of Mad Result.The next chapter has already begun.Capstone People ConsultingDr Sujaya Banerjee, CEODr Sujaya Banerjee, CEO Capstone People Consulting (Mumbai, Singapore) - is a much-admired Global Expert Educator in the Leadership and Culture space. She is associated with influencing leading organizations across the Globe through Duke Corporate Education and is a post Merger and Acquisition Integration Expert - Redefining Leadership, Culture Transformation and the Future of Work organizations across the world.In an era shaped by disruption, change and evolving workforce aspirations, Dr Sujaya stands out as one of Asia’s most influential voices in Leadership and Culture Transformation and is also a prominent Woman Leadership Thought Leader.Dr Banerjee has built a purpose-driven Consulting powerhouse that partners with 300 plus  leading organizations globally, regularly manifests reviews of 5/5 and has an NPS of 100. Under her leadership, Capstone is recognised for its Innovative solutions in the areas of Workplace Culture, Leadership Capability-Building, Executive Coaching, DEI and High-Performance transformation. Recognized as Forbes Most Valued Companies for 2025, Siliconindia’s Best OD & Leadership Development 2025, Top 10 Women to Follow in 2026, Dr Sujaya continues to redefine modern leadership, proving that organizations shaping the future are those that put People at the heart of transformation.The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: SwaLay Digital: Where music distribution meets ownership
description: An Indian company has created a complete rights protection system, including audio fingerprinting, content security, and government copyright registration, within a single music distribution platform. No Indian or global distributor has tried this on a large scale.SwaLay Digital, an Indian music distribution company operated by TalantonCore Group, is positioning itself as one of the world's most complete rights protection platforms for independent artists. It combines music distribution with its own content security technology and India's first built-in copyright registration tool. The company distributes music to over 50 global and Indian streaming platforms, including Spotify, Apple Music, JioSaavn, and Amazon Music. For the last two years, it has focused on building a rights framework entirely in-house. This system goes far beyond what any Indian distributor, and most global ones, currently provide.At the heart of this infrastructure is My D.N.A™️ (Digital Notarization & Authentication), a tool launched this year. It allows artists to file an official Indian copyright application without leaving the SwaLay platform. The process results in a copyright registration that is recognized under India's Copyright Act and the Information Technology Act. Artists can check the status of their application in real time from their existing portal."Copyright protection and distribution are the easy parts now," said Nikhil Jain, founder of SwaLay Digital. "The real problem nobody was solving is what happens after the music is out, who monitors for theft, who protects the rights, and who helps the artist truly own what they created. We built the company around that question."In addition to copyright registration, SwaLay has integrated ACRCloud and Shazam's audio fingerprinting service into its platform. This enables real-time detection of unauthorized use of an artist's music online, even if the music has been pitch-shifted, sped up, or partially reproduced. Audio files on the platform are available only through time-limited presigned URLs, which block direct downloads or redistributions by third parties. Each release automatically creates a verified SmartLink page, serving as a permanent public record of ownership.The combination of these tools, all developed and maintained in-house instead of relying on third-party services, places SwaLay in a small group of platforms worldwide that view rights protection as an essential aspect of distribution, not just an optional feature.This launch comes at a crucial time for India's independent music market. The country produced over 130,000 new releases in 2024, within an industry valued at around ₹2,100 crore. Most of those artists lack formal copyright protection for their work. Historically, the government registration process has required physical paperwork, legal expertise, and weeks of processing time, which are often beyond the reach of working musicians."An artist should be able to distribute their music, market it, and register the copyright all in one click from home," Jain stated. "That workflow didn't exist in India before we created it." SwaLay plans to expand its public rollout later in 2026, with international growth of its system aimed at Southeast Asia and the South Asian diaspora market.For more information, please visit: https://swalay.com/The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: IVAC marks major global wellness milestone with guests from 65+ countries
description: Indus Valley Ayurvedic Centre (IVAC), one of India’s most recognised Ayurvedic healing institutions, today announced a significant operational milestone: more than 50,000 guests have now completed therapeutic programmes at its Mysuru campus, with visitors recorded from over 65 countries across six continents. The centre also reports a guest satisfaction rating of 4.8 out of 5, based on independently verified reviews.The milestone positions IVAC among a small group of Ayurvedic institutions worldwide to have delivered physician-supervised classical treatments at this scale, and coincides with a broader shift in global wellness travel, from short-stay relaxation to structured, evidence-informed healing programmes.Milestone at a Glance50,000+ guests have completed healing programmes since the centre’s foundingGuests from 65+ countries, across Europe, the Americas, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East, and Africa8,000+ guests who have returned for repeat programmes, a metric the centre considers its core measure of outcome quality4.8 / 5 average satisfaction rating across independently submitted guest reviews25+ years of continuous physician-supervised Ayurvedic practice under a single founding medical director“This milestone is not simply a number. It is 50,000 individual decisions by people around the world to invest in their health through classical Ayurveda. Each guest arrived with a specific condition, a specific goal, or a specific need for restoration. That trust is what drives us to maintain the clinical rigour and personalisation that Ayurveda demands.”- Dr. Talavane Krishna, Founder and Medical Director, IVACWhy the Milestone Matters: A Shifting Global MarketThe timing of the announcement reflects a structural change in the global wellness industry. The Global Wellness Institute estimates the wellness economy at over USD 5.6 trillion, with wellness tourism emerging as one of its fastest-growing segments. Travellers are increasingly choosing destinations based on the clinical credibility and long-term health outcomes they offer, not merely the luxury of the experience.India, as the country of Ayurveda’s origin, holds a structural advantage in this environment. However, industry observers note that the advantage is only realised by institutions that deliver Ayurveda in its classical, physician-supervised form, a distinction that separates outcomes-focused centres from the broader ‘wellness retreat’ category.IVAC’s guest data, particularly the high proportion of guests from outside India and the volume of repeat visitors, is cited by the centre as evidence that physician-led classical Ayurveda commands an audience that purely spa-oriented offerings cannot easily replicate.About IVAC’s Clinical ModelFounded by Dr. Talavane Krishna, a qualified allopathic physician and anaesthesiologist who retrained in classical Ayurveda, IVAC operates on the principle that Ayurveda is most effective when delivered as a structured healing system rather than an experiential product. The centre’s approach begins with a detailed individual assessment, evaluating prakriti (constitutional type), current imbalances, and health history, before any therapy is prescribed.Panchakarma, the centre’s signature offering, is administered under the direct supervision of qualified Ayurvedic physicians and follows classical preparatory, treatment, and recovery protocols. The centre’s therapist training programme is conducted in-house to maintain consistency of technique and depth of knowledge across the guest experience.Located in Mysuru, Karnataka, a city with deep historical associations with yoga, Ayurveda, and traditional learning, IVAC benefits from a cultural environment in which Ayurvedic practice has existed continuously for centuries. The centre treats this context as a material part of the healing environment it offers guests.“Reaching 50,000 guests from over 65 countries is a meaningful data point for us, but the number we watch most closely is returning guests. More than 8,000 people have come back. In a category where the quality of outcomes is everything, repeat visits are the most honest signal we have.”-IVAC Guest Relations, Indus Valley Ayurvedic CentreWhat This Milestone Signals for Wellness Tourism in IndiaIVAC’s achievement is indicative of a broader opportunity for Karnataka and India. Mysuru, positioned as a hub for traditional knowledge systems and increasingly recognised on international wellness itineraries, is one of a small number of Indian cities with the cultural infrastructure, trained practitioner base, and institutional legacy to anchor a credible global wellness tourism brand.As interest in preventive health Ayurveda, chronic stress management, and non-pharmaceutical therapeutic approaches continues to grow internationally, authentic Ayurveda, delivered by institutions with the clinical depth and track record that IVAC represents , is well placed to serve a market that is both large and increasingly discerning.About Indus Valley Ayurvedic Centre (IVAC)Indus Valley Ayurvedic Centre is a physician-founded, physician-supervised Ayurvedic healing institution based in Mysuru, Karnataka, India. Established over 25 years ago by Dr. Talavane Krishna, IVAC specialises in classical Panchakarma therapy, personalised Ayurvedic treatment programmes, and preventive healthcare through authentic Vedic protocols. The centre has hosted more than 50,000 guests from over 65 countries and holds a 4.8-star guest satisfaction rating.The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: Your AI strategy is stalling in a place you're not looking: The data backlog
description: Most boards now have an AI strategy. Far fewer have noticed where it actually breaks.It rarely breaks at the model. It breaks several layers down, in the unglamorous work of getting clean, governed, current data to the place the model needs it. That work is still done largely by hand, one pipeline at a time, by a data team that was already at capacity before the AI mandate arrived. The strategy is sound. The supply chain feeding it isn't.For a Chief Data and Analytics Officer, this is the defining tension of 2026. Demand for data products is rising exponentially. The team building them grows, at best, in a straight line. No amount of executive urgency closes that gap, because it isn't a motivation problem. It's an architecture problem, and it deserves to be treated as one at the board level.The hidden tax on every initiativeConsider where a typical data team's hours actually go. Industry-wide, the majority of effort, often cited at 60 to 70%, goes into maintaining and fixing pipelines that already exist, not building the new ones the business is asking for. Every fire drill, every schema change that breaks a downstream report, every undocumented transformation someone has to reverse-engineer, is capacity quietly drained away from strategic work.This is the silent tax of the AI economy. It doesn't appear as a line item. It appears as a backlog that never shrinks, AI initiatives that slip another quarter, and a data leader increasingly cast as the gatekeeper of scarcity rather than the architect of advantage.Traditional automation was supposed to fix this, and it helped at the margins. But the integration platforms and workflow schedulers of the last decade automate tasks, not thinking. You still needed an engineer to design every pipeline, troubleshoot every failure, and rebuild every transformation when requirements changed. The work got faster. It didn't get smarter, and it didn't get smaller.A different equationThe shift now under way changes the equation rather than tuning it. The emerging category, AI Data Automation, moves the operational burden of data work, the building, testing, documenting, and maintaining, to autonomous AI agents, while keeping humans in command of strategy and governance. Maia, the platform built by data technology company Matillion, is one example of this approach in production.The distinction that matters for a CDAO is this: earlier tools made your existing team faster. This approach decouples output from headcount entirely. The volume of data work your organisation can deliver stops being a function of how many engineers you can hire and retain. For any leader who has tried to scale a specialist team in a competitive talent market, that is a structurally different proposition.The numbers from early adopters make the point concretely. A two-person data team at Edmund Optics, a global optics manufacturer with 34,000 product lines, used this model to revive a critical pipeline that had been stalled for a year and had already burned $50,000 in failed attempts. They rebuilt it in an afternoon, tripled their output, and saved $100,000 in consulting spend, with no new hires. At St. James's Place, a UK wealth management firm, an analysis process that consumed 4,000 hours a year was reduced to roughly 16 hours. Crucially, that happened inside a regulated financial services environment, with governance and audit standards intact.The governance question, answered properlyThat last point is where many executives, rightly, grow cautious. Autonomy is worthless to a serious enterprise if it comes at the cost of control. An AI system that builds pipelines quickly but ignores your compliance obligations or architecture standards is not an efficiency gain. It's a risk you've automated.The credible systems in this category are built to address exactly that. They operate within a governed foundation where every action is logged and every transformation is traceable. They capture business rules and governance requirements before executing, rather than guessing at them. This is what separates a genuine enterprise capability from an impressive demonstration, and it's the question a CDAO should press hardest in any evaluation. The right answer isn't "trust the AI." It's "here is the audit trail."What changes for the leaderHandled well, this does more than clear a backlog. It changes the strategic position of the data function itself.A CDAO spends much of their time today rationing. Which initiative gets resourced. Which request waits. Which modernisation effort is deferred so the lights stay on. The role becomes one of saying no, carefully and often. When the operational layer of data work is automated, that calculus shifts. Modernisation and new AI innovation can run in parallel rather than competing for the same scarce engineers. The default answer to a strategic request can move from "not this quarter" to "yes."The team changes with it. Engineers move off the treadmill of pipeline maintenance and onto data product ownership and architecture, the work that actually compounds in value. That's a more defensible team to build and a more attractive one to join, which matters in a market where data talent is hard to win and harder to keep.The board-level framingFor enterprises scaling AI ambitions against real talent constraints, the framing is worth getting right. The question is not "which data tool should we buy?" It's "what is the operating model for data work that lets our AI strategy actually execute?"Buying another tool that makes a stretched team marginally faster doesn't answer that question. Changing the model does. The organisations that will define the next decade aren't simply adopting AI features. They're rethinking, deliberately and at the top, how data work gets done at all.A detailed guide for data leaders on this shift is set out in this guide for CDAOs.Produced in partnership with Matillion, the data technology company behind the Maia AI data platform.The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: Cusack's Sheff Utd coach called her a 'psycho', inquest hears
description: Former Sheffield United midfielder Maddy Cusack's coach had called her a "psycho" and was playing mind games with her before her death, an inquest has heard.

title: Photo of the day: India's first bullet-train project finally picks up pace
description: This aerial photograph shows the construction site of the Mumbai–Ahmedabad High Speed Rail project in Maharashtra's Boisar on June 29, 2026. Spanning 508 km, the High-Speed Rail project is India's first bullet train. Powered by Japanese Shinkansen technology, the ₹1.1 lakh crore project is targeting an August 2027 launch.

title: शीनिंग: छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की दहलीज पर बसा आधुनिक शहर
description:
Qinghai-Xiaotang Plateau: अगर कोई आपसे पूछे कि छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की यात्रा कहां से शुरू होती है, तो जवाब होगा, शीनिंग। चीन के छिंगहाई प्रांत की राजधानी शीनिंग को यूं ही छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार का प्रवेश द्वार कहा जाता। सदियों से यह शहर लोगों, संस्कृतियों और व्यापारिक रास्तों को जोड़ता आया है। आज भी चीन के भीतरी.
The post शीनिंग: छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की दहलीज पर बसा आधुनिक शहर appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.
content:
Qinghai-Xiaotang Plateau: अगर कोई आपसे पूछे कि छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की यात्रा कहां से शुरू होती है, तो जवाब होगा, शीनिंग। चीन के छिंगहाई प्रांत की राजधानी शीनिंग को यूं ही छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार का प्रवेश द्वार कहा जाता। सदियों से यह शहर लोगों, संस्कृतियों और व्यापारिक रास्तों को जोड़ता आया है। आज भी चीन के भीतरी इलाकों को शीत्सांग से जोड़ने वाला यह सबसे महत्वपूर्ण परिवहन और पर्यटन केंद्र है। यही वजह है कि छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की ओर जाने वाले ज्यादातर यात्रियों का पहला पड़ाव शीनिंग ही होता है।
दरअसल, शीनिंग की यह पहचान कोई नई नहीं है। अगर इतिहास के पन्ने पलटें तो पता चलता है कि कभी यह शहर प्राचीन सिल्क रोड का एक अहम पड़ाव हुआ करता था। उस दौर में व्यापारियों के कारवां यहां से गुजरते थे और दूर-दूर तक सामान पहुंचाते थे। समय बदला, रास्ते बदले और परिवहन के साधन भी आधुनिक हो गए, लेकिन शीनिंग की अहमियत कभी कम नहीं हुई। आज यही शहर चीन के सबसे बड़े लॉजिस्टिक्स और कनेक्टिविटी केंद्रों में शामिल है। यही नहीं, शीनिंग केवल छिंगहाई प्रांत की प्रशासनिक राजधानी भर नहीं है। इसे पूरे छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार का सबसे बड़ा और प्रभावशाली शहर भी माना जाता है। राजनीति हो, व्यापार, शिक्षा, संस्कृति या फिर परिवहन, हर क्षेत्र में इसकी मजबूत मौजूदगी साफ दिखाई देती है। इसलिए पश्चिमी चीन के विकास की चर्चा शीनिंग के बिना अधूरी मानी जाती है।
अगर इसके भूगोल की बात करें तो शीनिंग की अपनी अलग खासियत है। यह शहर ह्वांगशुई नदी की घाटी में बसा है, जो ह्वांगहो यानी येलो रिवर की एक महत्वपूर्ण सहायक नदी है। समुद्र तल से लगभग 2,200 मीटर की ऊंचाई पर होने के कारण यहां का मौसम सालभर अपेक्षाकृत ठंडा और सुहावना बना रहता है। यही वजह है कि यहां पहुंचते ही वातावरण में एक अलग तरह की ताजगी महसूस होती है। लेकिन शीनिंग की पहचान केवल उसकी भौगोलिक स्थिति तक सीमित नहीं है। करीब 24 लाख की आबादी वाला यह शहर अपनी बहु-सांस्कृतिक विरासत के लिए भी जाना जाता है। यहां हान चीनी समुदाय के साथ-साथ हुई मुस्लिम, तिब्बती, सालार और थु समुदाय के लोग भी बड़ी संख्या में रहते हैं। यही विविधता इस शहर को खास बनाती है। यहां एक ओर मस्जिदें दिखाई देती हैं, तो दूसरी ओर बौद्ध मठ नजर आते हैं। वहीं पारंपरिक चीनी स्थापत्य कला भी हर कदम पर अपनी अलग छाप छोड़ती है।
शहर की सड़कों पर निकलते ही यह एहसास और गहरा हो जाता है। एक तरफ चौड़ी और आधुनिक सड़कें हैं, ऊंची इमारतें हैं, तो दूसरी तरफ पुराने बाजार और पारंपरिक जीवन की झलक भी दिखाई देती है। ऐसा लगता है जैसे शीनिंग ने अपनी ऐतिहासिक विरासत को संजोकर रखा है और साथ ही आधुनिक विकास को भी पूरे आत्मविश्वास के साथ अपनाया है। शायद यही संतुलन इसे दूसरे शहरों से अलग पहचान देता है। अब अगर अर्थव्यवस्था की बात करें तो यहां भी शीनिंग तेजी से आगे बढ़ता दिखाई देता है। ऊन, वस्त्र उद्योग, डेयरी उत्पाद और नमक उद्योग लंबे समय से यहां की अर्थव्यवस्था की मजबूत नींव रहे हैं। छिंगहाई पशुपालन और ऊन उत्पादन के लिए पहले से ही प्रसिद्ध रहा है। अब इन पारंपरिक उद्योगों को आधुनिक तकनीक, नए निवेश और बेहतर प्रबंधन के साथ जोड़ा जा रहा है, जिससे स्थानीय अर्थव्यवस्था को नई रफ्तार मिल रही है।
इसी विकास यात्रा को चीन की बेल्ट एंड रोड पहल ने भी नई दिशा दी है। आज शीनिंग से चलने वाली अंतरराष्ट्रीय मालगाड़ियां दुनिया के 70 से अधिक देशों तक सामान पहुंचा रही हैं। इसका सीधा फायदा यह हुआ कि शीनिंग अब केवल एक क्षेत्रीय शहर नहीं रहा, बल्कि वैश्विक व्यापार नेटवर्क का भी एक महत्वपूर्ण केंद्र बन चुका है। अगर परिवहन की बात करें तो शीनिंग की सबसे बड़ी ताकत उसकी बेहतरीन कनेक्टिविटी है। यहां का हवाई अड्डा पेइचिंग, शांगहाई, क्वांगचो सहित चीन के लगभग सभी प्रमुख शहरों से जुड़ा हुआ है। लेकिन शीनिंग की असली पहचान छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग रेलवे से बनी। इसी शहर से होकर गुजरने वाली इस ऐतिहासिक रेल लाइन ने आधुनिक रेल नेटवर्क को तिब्बती पठार तक पहुंचाया। यही कारण है कि ल्हासा जाने वाली अधिकांश ट्रेनें शीनिंग से होकर गुजरती हैं और यह शहर यात्रियों के लिए एक प्रमुख ट्रांजिट हब बन गया है।
अब अगर पर्यटन की बात करें तो शीनिंग का आकर्षण और भी बढ़ जाता है। यहां सिर्फ एक आधुनिक शहर नहीं, बल्कि प्रकृति, अध्यात्म, तिब्बती संस्कृति और ऊंचे पठारों की जीवनशैली का अनोखा संगम देखने को मिलता है। यही वजह है कि हर साल लाखों देसी और विदेशी पर्यटक यहां पहुंचते हैं। पिछले कुछ वर्षों में रोड ट्रिप, बाइकिंग और हाई एल्टीट्यूड एडवेंचर का शौक भी तेजी से बढ़ा है। कई पर्यटक तो केवल छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग राजमार्ग पर सफर करने का रोमांच महसूस करने के लिए ही शीनिंग आते हैं। मेरी नज़र में शीनिंग केवल एक शहर नहीं है। यह वह पुल है जो अतीत को भविष्य से जोड़ता है। यहां इतिहास की विरासत भी जीवित है और आधुनिक विकास की रफ्तार भी साफ दिखाई देती है। शायद यही वजह है कि जो भी छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की यात्रा पर निकलता है, उसकी कहानी की शुरुआत अक्सर शीनिंग से ही होती है।
(अखिल पाराशर, CGTN हिन्दी संवाददाता, बीजिंग)
The post शीनिंग: छिंगहाई-शीत्सांग पठार की दहलीज पर बसा आधुनिक शहर appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.

title: SpiceJet temporarily suspends services from Chennai airport
ജനങ്ങൾക്ക് കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പ് മുന്നറിയിപ്പ്, എൽനിനോ സാഹചര്യം തുടരുന്നു, കേരളത്തിൽ ജൂലൈയിലും മഴ കുറയും
description: സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് എൽനിനോ സാഹചര്യം തുടരുന്നതിനാൽ ജൂലൈ മാസത്തിലും സാധാരണയേക്കാൾ കുറഞ്ഞ മഴയ്ക്ക് സാധ്യതയെന്ന് കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പ്. അടുത്ത അഞ്ച് ദിവസത്തേക്ക് വിവിധ ജില്ലകളിൽ ഓറഞ്ച്, യെല്ലോ അലർട്ടുകൾ പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്.
content: <p><strong>തിരുവനന്തപുരം</strong> : സംസ്ഥാനത്ത്‌ ജൂലൈ മാസത്തിലും മുൻ വർഷങ്ങളെ അപേക്ഷിച്ച് സാധാരണ ലഭിക്കാവുന്ന മഴയേക്കാൾ കുറവ് മഴക്ക് സാധ്യതയെന്ന് കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പ് അറിയിപ്പ്. എൽനിനോ സാഹചര്യം തുടരുന്ന സാഹചര്യത്തിലാണ് സംസ്ഥാനത്ത് മഴ കുറയുന്നത്. തുടർന്നുള്ള കാലവർഷ മാസങ്ങളിൽ ഇത് കൂടുതൽ ശക്തി പ്രാപിക്കാൻ സാധ്യതയുണ്ടെന്നാണ് മുന്നറിയിപ്പ്.</p><p>അതേസമയം, ഇന്ത്യൻ ഓഷ്യൻ ഡൈപോൾ (Indian Ocean Dipole-IOD)ഇപ്പോൾ ന്യൂട്രൽ സ്ഥിതിയിൽ തുടരുകയാണ്. കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പിന്റെ ഉൾപ്പെടെയുള്ള ആഗോള കാലാവസ്ഥാ മോഡലുകളുടെ പ്രവചനപ്രകാരം, ന്യൂട്രൽ ഐഒഡി സ്ഥിതി മുഴുവൻ കാലവർഷ സീസണിലും തുടരാനാണ് സാധ്യത.</p><p><strong>അടുത്ത 5 ദിവസത്തേക്കുള്ള മഴ സാധ്യത</strong></p><p>കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പിന്റെ അടുത്ത 5 ദിവസത്തേക്കുള്ള മഴ സാധ്യത പ്രവചനമനുസരിച്ച് വിവിധ ജില്ലകളിൽ കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പ് ഓറഞ്ച്, മഞ്ഞ അലർട്ടുകൾ പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചിരിക്കുകയാണ്. ഇന്ന് കണ്ണൂർ, കാസറഗോഡ് ജില്ലകളിൽ ഓറഞ്ച് അലർട്ടാണ്. നാളെ കണ്ണൂർ, കാസറഗോഡ് ജില്ലകളിലും ഓറഞ്ച് അലർട്ടാണ്. ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ടയിടങ്ങളിൽ അതിശക്തമായ മഴയ്ക്കുള്ള സാധ്യതയാണ് പ്രവചിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നത്. പത്തനംതിട്ട, ആലപ്പുഴ, കോട്ടയം, ഇടുക്കി, എറണാകുളം, തൃശ്ശൂർ, പാലക്കാട്, മലപ്പുറം, കോഴിക്കോട്, വയനാട് ജില്ലകളിൽ ഇന്ന് യെല്ലോ അലർട്ടാണ്. നാളെ ആലപ്പുഴ, എറണാകുളം, തൃശ്ശൂർ, പാലക്കാട്, മലപ്പുറം, കോഴിക്കോട്, വയനാട് ജില്ലകളിൽ യെല്ലോ അലർട്ടാണ്.</p><p>02/07/2026: കോഴിക്കോട്, കണ്ണൂർ, കാസറഗോഡ്</p><p>03/07/2026: ഇടുക്കി, എറണാകുളം, തൃശ്ശൂർ, മലപ്പുറം, കോഴിക്കോട്, കണ്ണൂർ, കാസറഗോഡ്</p><p>04/07/2026: പത്തനംതിട്ട, കോട്ടയം, ഇടുക്കി, എറണാകുളം, തൃശ്ശൂർ, മലപ്പുറം, കോഴിക്കോട്, കണ്ണൂർ, കാസറഗോഡ് എന്നീ ജില്ലകളിൽ കേന്ദ്ര കാലാവസ്ഥ വകുപ്പ് മഞ്ഞ അലർട്ട് പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നു.</p><h2>പൊതുജനങ്ങൾക്കുള്ള ജാഗ്രത നിർദേശം</h2><p>ഉരുൾപൊട്ടൽ, മണ്ണിടിച്ചിൽ, മലവെള്ളപ്പാച്ചിൽ സാധ്യതയുള്ള പ്രദേശങ്ങളിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നവർ അധികൃതരുടെ നിർദേശാനുസരണം സുരക്ഷിതമായ സ്ഥലങ്ങളിലേക്ക് മാറി താമസിക്കണം.</p><p>നദിക്കരകൾ, അണക്കെട്ടുകളുടെ കീഴ്പ്രദേശങ്ങൾ എന്നിവിടങ്ങളിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നവരും അപകടസാധ്യത മുൻകൂട്ടി കണ്ട് അധികൃതരുടെ നിർദേശാനുസരണം മാറി താമസിക്കേണ്ടതാണ്.</p><p>ദുരന്തസാധ്യത പ്രദേശങ്ങളിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നവർ നിർബന്ധമായും തങ്ങളുടെ പ്രദേശത്ത് ക്യാമ്പുകൾ തുറന്നു എന്നുറപ്പാക്കേണ്ടതും പകൽ സമയത്ത് തന്നെ അങ്ങോട്ട് മാറി താമസിക്കേണ്ടതുമാണ്. ഇതിനായി തദ്ദേശ സ്ഥാപന, റവന്യൂ അധികാരികളുമായി ബന്ധപ്പെടാവുന്നതാണ്.</p><p>ശക്തമായ കാറ്റിന് സാധ്യതയുള്ളതിനാൽ അടച്ചുറപ്പില്ലാത്ത വീടുകളിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നവരും മേൽക്കൂര ശക്തമല്ലാത്ത വീടുകളിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നവരും പ്രത്യേക ജാഗ്രത പാലിക്കേണ്ടതാണ്. അപകടാവസ്ഥ മുന്നിൽ കാണുന്നവർ അധികൃതരുമായി ബന്ധപ്പെട്ട് സുരക്ഷിതമായ സ്ഥലങ്ങളിലേക്ക് സുരക്ഷാ മുൻകരുതലിന്റെ ഭാഗമായി മാറി താമസിക്കണം.</p><p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Why does it say 'Braut Haaland' on the back of Erling Haaland's Norway jersey?
description: The Manchester City star has two names on the back of his jersey at the World Cup.
content: Manchester City star Erling Haaland is playing in his first World Cup and helped Norway easily reach the knockout rounds for the first time since 1998.But when it comes to Haaland's jersey, the star striker is sporting a different name than what Premier League fans are used to seeing from the Norwegian. While the back on Haaland's jersey at Manchester City just has the name "Haaland," his national team shirt displays "Braut Haaland."There's a reason for that. Haaland has wanted to honor both of his parents at this historic World Cup. Haaland is his father's last name. Braut is his mother's maiden name. Erling's mother, Gry Marita Braut, competed for Norway as a heptathlon champion. So, displaying her name with the national team has extra significance to both Erling and Norway. Erling Haaland & His mother #MCFCpic.twitter.com/StngRfdNqc— Out Of Context Erling Haaland (@ErlingNoContext) October 19, 2023This article originally appeared on For The Win: Why does it say 'Braut Haaland' on the back of Erling Haaland's Norway jersey?

title: VB-G RAM G Act to come into force from July 1; Centre says all ready for rollout
5 reasons why Yankees are absolutely brutal right now | Klapisch
description: The Bombers have been held to 12 runs in their current five-game losing streak.
content: NEW YORK — Imagine a world where the scaffolding of the Yankees’ success has collapsed. Nothing is working. Nothing feels right. One loss blurs into the next with no end in sight.The Bombers’ five-game losing streak, a season high, isn’t going to keep them out of the playoffs, but the vibe is nevertheless terrible. The question on everyone’s mind: What’s gone wrong with this team?Manager Aaron Boone said, “we have to play better” but that sounds more like a plea for help than a solution. The Yankees have surrendered first place in the East to the Rays, which makes next week’s four-game series in St. Petersburg all the more important.The Yankees aren’t in danger of becoming the free-falling Mets. But the series of setbacks, including Monday’s 7-3 loss to Detroit, reveal just how fragile the Bombers are when a few key components go offline.Here’s what’s going wrong:LIFE WITHOUT AARON JUDGEThe Yankees actually went on a five-game winning streak soon after the captain went on the Injured List with a fractured rib. For that, Boone could thank Ben Rice and Cody Bellinger.But both sluggers are practically invisible now. Rice is batting .103 in his last 10 games. He has exactly one RBI in that span – a home run. Bellinger is 5-for-41 in his last 12 games and zero RBIs. “Obviously, I need to be better,” he said. “I am not playing as well as I can … I have to produce.”Former Yankee slugger and current YES analyst Paul O’Neill pulled Bellinger aside during batting practice on Monday to offer this nugget:“I see (Bellinger) drifting forward, which is why I’d like to see him use a leg kick,” said O’Neill, the American League’s batting champion in 1994, “It’s what I did to help my timing. It forces you to stay back.”Bellinger admitted, “we’re both tall and tall guys tend to jump too far forward,” but brushed aside any sort of experiment. “I’ve never had a leg kick in my whole life, you know,” he said. “I’m not going to implement a leg kick.”BRUTAL DEFENSERemember how the Dodgers mocked the Yankees for their sloppy play in the 2024 World Series? It wasn’t just the Dodgers who felt that way. The entire industry believed the Yankees’ defense would ultimately crack under pressure.That same criticism is again valid. After allowing 20 unearned runs in the first 74 games, the Yankees have yielded the same number in the last 10 – including eight in the four-game sweep at Fenway over the weekend.Even Bellinger, arguably the game’s most gifted leftfielder, has been affected. He dropped Dillon Dingler’s fly ball in the corner in the fourth inning, which helped the Tigers to a 7-0 lead that effectively ended the game.Bellinger sheepishly admitted, “I just closed my glove too soon.”The sloppiness didn’t end there. In the very next at-bat, Hao-Yu Lee’s fly ball to shallow right resulted in a horrific collision between Jazz Chisholm and Jasson Dominguez.Neither defender swerved to avoid each other. Instead, Dominguez’s elbow caught Chisholm’s flush on the jaw. He crashed to the ground and remained there for several moments while being attended to by the Yankees’ trainers.Dominguez ruefully said, “I called (Chisholm) off but the crowd was too loud. He couldn’t hear me.”Chisholm walked off the field under his own power and underwent concussion protocol. The Yankees didn’t update the second baseman’s condition after the game.ALL THOSE STRANDED RUNNERS:Remove Judge, Bellinger, Rice and - to a lesser degree - Paul Goldschmidt, whose average has dropped 17 points this week, and there aren’t many bullets left in the Yankees’ chamber.The Yankees are batting .175 in the last 10 games. According to the YES Network, the Bombers are 6-for-65 with runners in scoring position. They’ve been stymied recently by Detroit’s Casey Mize (10 strikeouts), Boston’s Sonny Gray (no-hitter into the eighth inning) and Boston’s Payton Tolle (seven shutout innings).What the Yankees need, of course, is Judge’s massive presence in the lineup. It’s not just about protecting Rice in the No. 3 spot; it’s the pervasive threat that the Bombers’ most powerful hitter generates.Pitchers are terrified of Judge’s potential for late-inning home runs that break hearts. The Yankees faked it for a while, but the illusion has now been broken.A WOBBLY ROTATIONThe Yankees are lucky to have Cam Schlittler as a temporary ace. He’s the only starter they could trust tonight to oppose Tarik Skubal, the Tigers’ superstar No. 1.But the Bombers were hoping Gerrit Cole’s miracle comeback from Tommy John surgery was indeed real. Cole was – and still is – the Yankees’ most accomplished asset at the top of the rotation.But Cole has allowed 17 earned runs in his last 25 innings, and says, “the performance on some of the pitches just hasn’t been good.” The trickle-down effect has been undeniable: the Yankees have had one quality start in the last 10 games. Ryan Weathers didn’t help matters by getting knocked out in the second inning on Monday, allowing five runs.THE VIBEIt says a lot that the ticket buyers voted with their feet on Monday. A migration of thousands started heading for the exits by the seventh inning. MORE BY BOB KLAPISCHThe intriguing Yankees storyline we could miss if a lockout cancels baseball in 2027 | KlapischJun. 26, 2026, 7:13 a.m.Yankees’ last-minute pitching change raises eyebrows: Is it a concern? | KlapischJun. 22, 2026, 2:23 p.m.The Yankees were just as lifeless as the crowd. Nothing feels the same lately. Judge’s return will likely mean a huge shift in organizational momentum. But who knows when that will be?The club said Judge would undergo new imaging on the fractured rib within four weeks after his diagnosis on June 4. That was 26 days ago and no one’s said a word about Judge’s progress.Read the original article on NJ.com. Add NJ.com as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

title: Which teams have been eliminated from the World Cup? Full list of misery
description: The list of World Cup teams still playing is dwindling. Here's the current list of squads that have been sent home.
content: ATLANTA ― The 2026 World Cup continues to deliver.This year's tournament has been fantastic. With the expanded 48-team field, there have been so many good games from the opening kickoff, and the action hasn't stopped. On Monday, Paraguay stunned Germany in a penalty kick thriller. Then Morocco and the Netherlands also went into penalty kicks – because why not? – setting up a dramatic finish.The round of 32 action continues on Tuesday, June 30 with the Ivory Coast battling Norway, France taking on Sweden and Mexico versus Ecuador in a World Cup nightcap. With so many fantastic wins and losses, it can be hard to keep up with which teams are still playing and which have been sent home. title: monsoon rain | జూలైలో సాధారణం కంటే తక్కువ వర్షపాతమే.. ఐఎండీ అంచనా
description: monsoon rain : దేశంలో ఇప్పటికే వర్షపాతం లోటు కనిపిస్తోంది. జూన్‌లో తగినంతగా వర్షాలు కురవలేదు. జూలైలోనైనా భారీ వర్షాలు కురుస్తాయని ఆశిస్తున్న వారికి షాకిచ్చే ప్రకటన చేసింది భారత వాతావరణ సంస్థ ఐఎండీ. జూలైలో సాధారణం కంటే తక్కువ వర్షపాతమే నమోదవుతుందని ప్రకటించింది.
content:
monsoon rain : దేశంలో ఇప్పటికే వర్షపాతం లోటు కనిపిస్తోంది. జూన్‌లో తగినంతగా వర్షాలు కురవలేదు. జూలైలోనైనా భారీ వర్షాలు కురుస్తాయని ఆశిస్తున్న వారికి షాకిచ్చే ప్రకటన చేసింది భారత వాతావరణ సంస్థ ఐఎండీ. జూలైలో సాధారణం కంటే తక్కువ వర్షపాతమే నమోదవుతుందని ప్రకటించింది. పసిఫిక్ భూమధ్య రేఖ వద్ద ఎల్‌నినో బలపడటం వల్ల ఈ పరిస్థితి ఉంటుందని ఐఎండీ తెలిపింది. ఇప్పటికే జూన్‌లో 39 శాతం తక్కువ వర్షపాతం నమోదైంది.
1901 తర్వాత ఈ స్థాయిలో జూన్‌లో లోటు వర్షపాతం నమోదు కావడం ఇది ఐదోసారి. అంటే 125 ఏళ్లలో ఇంత తక్కువ వర్షపాతం నమోదైన ఐదు జూన్‌లలో ఇదీ ఒకటి. ఐఎండీ తాజా అంచనా ప్రకారం జూలైలో దేశంలోని అనేక ప్రాంతాల్లో తక్కువ వర్షపాతమే నమోదవుతుంది. సెంట్రల్ ఇండియా, ఉత్తర భారత, తూర్పు భారత్ ప్రాంతాల్లో లోటువర్షపాతమే ఉంటుంది. కొన్ని ప్రాంతాల్లో మాత్రం భారీ వర్షపాతమే నమోదయ్యే ఛాన్స్ ఉంది. వాయువ్య, తూర్పు-మధ్య, తూర్పు ప్రాంతాల్లో సాధారణం నుంచి భారీ వర్షాలు కురిసే ఛాన్స్ ఉంది. దేశంలో లోటు వర్షపాతం ఉండటంతో వ్యవసాయ రంగంలో అనిశ్చితి నెలకొంది. గత ఏడాదితో పోలిస్తే ఖరీఫ్ సాగు 23 శాతం తగ్గినట్లు కేంద్రం అంచనా వేస్తోంది. వర్షాలు కురవకపోవడంతో వరి, పప్పుధాన్యాలు, సోయాబీన్, పత్తి వంటి పంటలు ఎక్కువగా నాట్లు వేయలేదు.
వర్షాకాలంలో నాలుగు నెలల్లో జూన్, జూలైలలో తక్కువ వర్షపాతమే నమోదవుతుంది. అయితే, ఆ తర్వాత నుంచి భారీ వర్షాలు కురిసే ఛాన్స్ ఉంది. ఆగష్టు, సెప్టెంబర్‌లలో మెరుగైన వర్షపాతం నమోదు కావొచ్చని ఆస్ట్రేలియాకు చెందిన వాతావరణ సంస్థ బ్యూరో ఆఫ్ మెటియోరాలజీ (బీఓఎం) తెలిపింది. కానీ, జూలైలో నమోదయ్యే వర్షపాతమే దేశంలో భారీ వర్షాలకు కారణమవుతుంది. వర్షాల లోటును తీరుస్తుంది. ఇప్పటికే దేశంలోని పలు ప్రాంతాల్లో వర్షాలు పడుతున్నా.. సాధారణ వర్షపాతమే నమోదవుతోంది.

title: College Football 27 features a Florida team rated in the top 20
description: EA Sports rated Florida No. 19 overall in College Football 27 with player ratings led by star RB Jadan Baugh and WR Eric Singleton Jr.
content: Florida football has 28 players rated at 80 or above after EA Sports College Football 27 revealed its team and player rankings this month. On3's Zach Abolverdi detailed what EA "got right — and wrong" when framing the Gators.EA lists star running back Jadan Baugh as Florida's highest-rated player at 91 overall, followed by wide receiver Eric Singleton Jr., who was rated 90. The Georgia Tech transfer's 95 speed attribute makes him Florida's most explosive weapon in CFB 27.The Gators' top defensive player is defensive back Bryce Thornton, who earned an 87 overall. Other highly-rated defenders include linebacker Myles Graham (85) and edge rusher Kamran James (84). "He’s tied with defensive tackle Brendan Bett (82 overall) for the most improved player rating in the game. Thornton and Bett both have a 10-point increase from last year when they were rated 77 and 72 overall, respectively," Abolverdi says of the ratings."While you can make an argument for Myles Graham or Jayden Woods to be the top-rated defender, EA Sports giving that designation to Thornton makes sense. He’s the highest graded returner on defense, according to PFF, and has started 17 consecutive games for UF, more than anyone else except left guard Knijeah Harris (25). EA Sports likely references and values PFF grades in its player ratings," he goes on to say.When discussing the most improved players for Florida, Abolverdi said, " I give EA Sports credit for moving up Bett to 82 overall. According to the staff, his offseason was fantastic and he has emerged as not only a starting defensive lineman, but one of the leaders in the locker room. Head strength coach Rusty Whitt identified Woods, Baugh and Bett as the three standouts from winter workouts."The Gators are slated 84 overall, with a balanced score of 84 on offense and 83 on defense — Jon Sumrall's team landed at No. 19. EA confirms what many analysts and experts have been suggesting all offseason, that Florida has enough talent to compete immediately in what some felt would be a rebuilding season in Gainesville.Follow us @GatorsWire on X, formerly known as Twitter, as well as Bluesky, and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of Florida Gators news, notes and opinions.This article originally appeared on Gators Wire: College Football 27 features a Florida team rated in the top 20

title: Venezuela Bond Rally Fades as Investors Price in Bleaker Outlook

title: EPFO News: All services back from July 1 after 5-day system upgrade; claims and passbook access restored
description: EPFO News: ​​The EPFO had temporarily suspended key services, including claim submissions, passbook downloads, and access to the Member Portal, Employer Portal, and UMANG app, from June 26 to June 30, 2026.
content: EPFO News: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) will restore all online member services from Wednesday, July 1, 2026, following the completion of a five-day system upgrade and database migration.Services affected during maintenanceThe EPFO had temporarily suspended key services, including claim submissions, passbook downloads, and access to the Member Portal, Employer Portal, and UMANG app, from June 26 to June 30, 2026. The maintenance was undertaken to consolidate databases and upgrade the organisation’s digital infrastructure.Also Read | ITR filing: How to file ITR without Form 16 using AIS and Form 26AS? - Check hereWhat did EPFO say?bIn an official post on X, the EPFO stated that the upgraded platform is expected to deliver faster, more reliable, and secure services once the transition is complete.The retirement fund body also sent SMS alerts to subscribers, saying, "Member services such as online claim submission and passbook downloads will be temporarily unavailable… Services are expected to be restored from July 1, 2026. We regret the inconvenience caused."Also Read | Govt keeps interest rates on small savings schemes unchanged for Q2 FY27 - DetailsThis exercise is part of EPFO’s ongoing efforts to modernise its technology infrastructure and improve user experience for its over 7 crore members. Officials expect the new system to significantly enhance the speed and efficiency of online services.Subscribers who postponed filing claims or downloading their passbooks during this period can now proceed once the services go live on July 1. Normal operations are expected to resume smoothly from the next day.

title: ‘రాజు వెడ్స్ రాంబాయి’ హీరో అఖిల్ రాజ్ ఏమన్నారంటే?
description: Delivery Boy Controversy : సోషల్ మీడియాలో వైరల్ అవుతున్న డెలివరీ బాయ్‌పై దాడి వివాదంపై ‘రాజు వెడ్స్ రాంబాయి’ చిత్ర హీరో అఖిల్ రాజ్ తాజాగా...
content:
Delivery Boy Controversy : సోషల్ మీడియాలో వైరల్ అవుతున్న డెలివరీ బాయ్‌పై దాడి వివాదంపై ‘రాజు వెడ్స్ రాంబాయి’ చిత్ర హీరో అఖిల్ రాజ్ తాజాగా క్లారిటీ ఇచ్చారు. తాము ఆర్డర్ చేయని ఒక పార్శిల్‌ను డెలివరీ బాయ్ తమ ఇంటికి తీసుకువచ్చాడని, పొరపాటు గ్రహించక తన తల్లి ఆ పార్శిల్‌ను పక్కన పెట్టిందని ఆయన వివరించారు. అయితే, దాదాపు 20 నిమిషాల తర్వాత తిరిగి వచ్చిన డెలివరీ బాయ్.. తన తల్లిని ఉద్దేశించి “ఏందే ఫ్రీగా వస్తే తీసుకుంటారా?” అంటూ చాలా దురుసుగా, అవమానకరంగా మాట్లాడాడని అఖిల్ రాజ్ పేర్కొన్నారు. కన్నతల్లిని అంతటి మాటలు అంటే ఏ కొడుకైనా కోప్పడటం సహజమని, ఆ క్షణంలో మాటల యుద్ధం జరిగిందే తప్ప తాను అతనిపై ఎలాంటి భౌతిక దాడి చేయలేదని స్పష్టం చేశారు.



Read Also : రాజకీయాల్లోకి సూర్య వస్తున్నారా?



Hero Akhil Raj Controversy







“నువ్వు హీరో కదా.. వీడియో వైరల్ చేస్తా” అంటూ బెదిరింపులు!



సదరు డెలివరీ బాయ్ కావాలనే వివాదాన్ని పెద్దది చేశాడని, అఖిల్ రాజ్ సెలబ్రిటీ అనే విషయాన్ని వాడుకుంటూ బ్లాక్‌మెయిలింగ్‌కు దిగాడని హీరో ఆరోపించారు. “నువ్వు హీరో కదా.. నీ వీడియో తీసి సోషల్ మీడియాలో వైరల్ చేస్తా” అంటూ అక్కడ పెద్ద హంగామా సృష్టించాడని అఖిల్ రాజ్ ఒక వీడియో ప్రకటన ద్వారా వెల్లడించారు. నిజానిజాలు తెలుసుకోకుండా కేవలం ఒకవైపు మాటలు నమ్మి సోషల్ మీడియాలో తప్పుడు ప్రచారం చేయవద్దని ఆయన నెటిజన్లను కోరారు. ఈ వివాదానికి సంబంధించిన పూర్తి వివరాలను, తన వెర్షన్‌ను స్పష్టంగా వివరిస్తూ ఆయన సోషల్ మీడియా వేదికగా ఈ వీడియోను పోస్ట్ చేయడంతో ప్రస్తుతం ఈ అంశం టాలీవుడ్ వర్గాల్లో హాట్ టాపిక్‌గా మారింది.

title: What Are Red Sox's Postseason Chances After Five Game Win Streak?
description: The Boston Red Sox got off to a very slow start to the season, but are still hanging around in a very weak American League.
content:
What Are Red Sox's Postseason Chances After Five Game Win Streak? originally appeared on NESN.
                Add NESN as a Preferred Source by clicking here.The Boston Red Sox got off to a very slow start to the season, but are still hanging around in a very weak American League.On the eve of July 1, only five teams in the AL have a winning record. The Red Sox sit 4 1/2 games out of a wild card spot, with five teams ahead of them. There is still plenty of season left, and the Red Sox have some hope after showing life and winning five straight games, including a four-game sweep of the New York Yankees.The Red Sox still have plenty of ground to make up, but their postseason chances have gone from 9.7% on June 18 to 20.6% entering play on Tuesday, according to FanGraphs.While their chances are still slim, they have a few reasons to believe they can make a push. For one, the starting rotation has been lights out. Ranger Suarez, Payton Tolle and Sonny Gray have all been phenomenal, and Garrett Crochet should return at some point.Additionally, the offense could get Roman Anthony, Trevor Story and Romy González back at some point. González is more of a utility player, and Story was struggling before his injury, but Anthony could genuinely turn the entire season around. He was not playing great before his injury, but he is the face of the franchise now and has the potential to go on a tear.He was amazing last year and was a superstar in the World Baseball Classic. If he can come back and provide some more power, the Red Sox could be set up with elite pitching and a scary offense.More MLB: Caleb Durbin's Numbers Starting To Make Red Sox Look Good For Letting Alex Bregman Walk

title: ഇടുക്കിയിൽ റിസോർട്ടിൽ നാല് വയസുകാരന് ക്രൂരമർദനം, പിന്നിൽ ചെന്നൈയിൽ നിന്നെത്തിയ സഞ്ചാരികൾ, അക്രമികളെ തടഞ്ഞുവെച്ച് നാട്ടുകാർ
description: പുറത്തേക്ക് കൊണ്ടുപോയി കുട്ടിയെ വലിച്ചെറിയുകയും ചവിട്ടുകയും ചെയ്തു. ആക്രമിച്ചവരെ നാട്ടുകാർ തടഞ്ഞു വെച്ചിരിക്കുകയാണ്. മർദ്ദനത്തിന്റെ സിസിടിവി ദൃശ്യങ്ങൾ പുറത്ത് വന്നിട്ടുണ്ട്.
content: <p>ഇടുക്കി: ഇടുക്കി കാന്തല്ലൂരിൽ റിസോർട്ടിൽ നാല് വയസുകാരന് മർദനം. ചെന്നൈയിൽ നിന്നെത്തിയ സഞ്ചാരികളാണ് കുട്ടിയെ ആക്രമിച്ചത്. ഇവരുടെ തന്നെ സംഘത്തിൽ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്ന കുട്ടിയുടെ നേർക്ക് തന്നെയാണ് ആക്രമണം നടന്നിരിക്കുന്നത്. പുറത്തേക്ക് കൊണ്ടുപോയി കുട്ടിയെ വലിച്ചെറിയുകയും ചവിട്ടുകയും ചെയ്തു. ആക്രമിച്ചവരെ നാട്ടുകാർ തടഞ്ഞു വെച്ചിരിക്കുകയാണ്. മർദ്ദനത്തിന്റെ സിസിടിവി ദൃശ്യങ്ങൾ പുറത്ത് വന്നിട്ടുണ്ട്.</p><p></p>

title: 2026 Philadelphia Eagles single-game, public training camp tickets go on sale
description: Single-game tickets for 2026 Philadelphia Eagles home games and the team's public training camp went on sale Tuesday morning.
content: Get ready to fly high, Eagles fans! It won't be long until Lincoln Financial Field is once again hosting a sea of green on Sundays.And fans now have the chance to secure a spot in the crowd.Eagles single-game tickets went on sale through Ticketmaster at 10 a.m. on Tuesday.There is a four-ticket limit per household.Fans can also grab tickets for the team's public training camp practice on August 25.General admission tickets are $15. There will also be an option to purchase a $50 VIP ticket that will give fans a special on-field experience before the start of practice.All proceeds from those tickets will benefit the Eagles Autism Foundation.

title: 2026 Open Championship final qualifying leaderboard, live updates, scores, highlights
description: Final qualifying will begin with 288 golfers are four locations battling to be one of the final 20 left standing who will advance to the British Open.
content: From 288 to 20. Final qualifying for the 2026 British Open will have 288 players at four locations, with only the top five from each moving on to the final men's major of the season.The full field for the Open will be 156 (there are currently 112 exempt players) when it starts on July 16 and the 36-hole final qualifying will be the final chance for those lucky 20. There are four locations, three in England and one in Scotland, to determine who gets spots on the tee sheet at Royal Birkdale.For live leaderboard updates and highlights throughout the day, keep it right here with Golfweek.2026 Open Championship final qualifying leaderboardsKeep tabs on the scores as they come in at the four qualifying sites:Burnham & BerrowDundonald LinksRoyal Cinque PortsWest LancashireThe Bryan Bros. are aiming for Royal BirkdaleThe pursuit continues. George and Wesley Bryan are making their move in round two of Final Qualifying at West Lancashire, spots at The 154th Open firmly in their sight. Follow live: https://t.co/TGg4psVQwgpic.twitter.com/6KPLr59gZD— The Open (@TheOpen) June 30, 2026Where to watch the Open Championship final qualifyingFor the first time ever, the R&A will live stream final qualfying, doing so on its YouTube channel. Futher, the R&A will have a live final qualifying radio broadcast. Both platforms will start coverage at 4:30 p.m. UK time, which is 11:30 a.m. ET.Who's already exempt into the 2026 Open Championship?Through one or more of the 21 ways to earn an exemption, there are already 122 golfers in the field.Who's trying to advance through qualifying for the 2026 Open?First, a name backed out of final qualifying: Dustin Johnson, who made the cut in the first three majors and had a brief moment near the top of the leaderboard at the U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills. He was originally set for final qualifying for the Open but has since withdrawn. Some notables who are on the tee sheets: Sergio Garcia, James Nicholas, Marc Leishman, Luke Poulter (a), Thomas Detry, Branden Grace, George Bryan, Wesley Bryan, Danny Willett and Matt Wallace.This article originally appeared on Golfweek: 2026 Open Championship final qualifying leaderboard, live updates, scores, highlights

title: Tyron Woodley: Conor McGregor needs to come forward, frustrate Max Holloway
description: Tyron Woodley advises Conor McGregor to go right at Max Holloway.
content: Tyron Woodley advises Conor McGregor to go right at Max Holloway.McGregor (22-6 MMA, 10-4 UFC) returns from a five-year long layoff to run things back with Holloway (27-9 MMA, 23-9 UFC) in the UFC 329 main event on July 11 at T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas (Paramount+).McGregor won their first outing at featherweight in 2013, but the pair will now rematch at welterweight. Despite the injury layoff, McGregor is coming into the bout oozing with the same confidence he always has, which Woodley thinks could play a factor."If Conor comes forward, gets into Max's head and lands some shots, then frustrates him, he has a good chance," Woodley said in an interview with Betpack. "But Max has always had a good chance. He is tough, he loves fighting those fights, his cardio is great, his skill set is great, and he just looks like he belongs out there."Holloway is a moderate favorite over McGregor, and former UFC welterweight champion Woodley ultimately thinks the oddsmakers have got it right."I think Max is going to win," Woodley said. "I'm not going for Conor, but if I was somebody involved in his process to get ready for this fight, I would have him train with young, hungry, spunky fighters. Conor said something about Ilia (Topuria). "He said, 'You train with these people that can't even beat you, and you are not getting better.' He said, 'You need to put $1,000 down and say, whoever can knock me out gets $1,000.' Basically, what he said is that you have to be in a room where somebody is pushing you. I agree with that."This article originally appeared on MMA Junkie: Tyron Woodley: Conor McGregor needs to come forward, frustrate Max Holloway

title: Alpha CENSORED - CBFC Asks Alia Bhatt-Sharvari Film To Make Changes In Disclaimer, Violent Visuals
description: Alpha has received a UA 16+ certificate from the CBFC, which asked the makers to modify the disclaimer, reduce certain violent visuals and delete a foul word. Starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, the YRF Spy film releases on July 3.
content: With just days left for its theatrical release, Alpha has completed its censor formalities. The much-awaited YRF Spy Universe film starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari has received a UA 16+ certificate from the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC). However, before granting certification, the board asked the makers to make a few modifications, primarily concerning violent visuals, disclaimers, and certain technical requirements. Despite being an action-heavy spy thriller, the CBFC has reportedly demanded only limited changes, allowing the film to retain most of its original vision.CBFC asks Alpha makers to modify disclaimerAs per Bollywood Hungama, one of the primary changes suggested by the CBFC involves the disclaimer shown at the beginning of the film. According to reports, the Examining Committee asked the makers to redraft the disclaimer before Alpha  begins.Apart from this, the board also instructed the makers to ensure that the opening cards and end credits appear in the applied language, which in this case is Hindi. Additionally, subtitles have reportedly been inserted for all songs featured in the film to comply with certification requirements.  Violent scenes, foul words removed from AlphaWhile Alpha largely escaped major cuts, the CBFC did ask the makers to tone down certain violent sequences.Reports suggest that visuals from one scene in the first half of the film were reduced and replaced with alternative shots. Another sequence in the first half also underwent a similar modification.The most significant change reportedly came in the second half, where scenes involving multiple stabbings were asked to be toned down. The board directed the makers to reduce the intensity of these visuals and replace them with alternative shots. However, specific details regarding the scenes or characters involved have not been disclosed. Apart from the violent visuals, the CBFC also directed the makers to remove a foul word from the first half of the film.The Examining Committee also instructed the makers to include anti-alcohol static warnings in scenes featuring alcohol consumption. Additionally, the makers were asked to submit the mandatory parental consent documents for the child artist featured in the film.  More about AlphaAlong with the certification, Alpha has locked its runtime at 140 minutes, making it a two-hour-and-twenty-minute action thriller. The CBFC certificate also revealed the film's official plot summary, revealing a bit about Alia Bhatt's character, Sita. According to the synopsis, Sita is a woman driven by revenge who destroys anyone standing in her way while uncovering hidden truths about her own life. The character is described as bold, emotionally detached, and willing to cross moral boundaries to achieve her goals.Directed by Shiv Rawail, Alpha also stars Sharvari, Bobby Deol, and Anil Kapoor in pivotal roles. The film holds special significance as it marks the first female-led chapter in the highly successful YRF Spy Universe, which includes blockbuster franchises such as Tiger, War, and Pathaan.

title: Can the World Cup's viral stars turn social media fame into fortunes?
description: They’re not Ronaldo or Messi, but they might be set for life after gaining millions of new followers.
content: Cape Verde's Vozinha celebrates with fans [Reuters]It took 90 minutes for Vozinha, Cape Verde's 40-year-old goalkeeper, to become a world-wide sensation with more Instagram followers than NFL legend Tom Brady.Vozinha's impressive performance against Spain in the World Cup group stage led to a 0-0 draw, a huge surprise against one of the tournament favourites. It led to Vozinha's 50,000 Instagram followers ballooning to 17.4 million, surpassing athletes such as Brady (15.5 million).World Cup stars, like Vozinha, can harness their new social media fame to generate lucrative financial opportunities. But media expert Mike Serazio said those prospects can be fleeting. "It's viral - it goes up very fast and it goes down equally fast," he said.Brooke Duffy, a digital and social media scholar at Cornell University, said influencers with millions of followers can demand payments that stretch beyond six-figures.A prominent social media presence can lead to brand partnerships and advertisers paying for individual posts. "Followers are a form of currency that matters now… more followers tends to translate into a higher income," she said. Social media offers a different path to stardomTim Payne, a defender on the New Zealand team, was dubbed the "least-known" World Cup player by one Argentine influencer ahead of the tournament. Valen Scarsini, known as 'elscarso' online, shared a video calling on his hundreds of thousands of followers to help boost Payne's profile. Payne leaned into this, posting more and engaging with the influencer. Over a few days, Payne's Instagram follower count grew from about 5,000 to nearly six million. He has a larger following, he pointed out, than the population of the country he hails from (New Zealand's population is just over 5.3 million).Unlike in the case of Cape Verde's Vozinha, Payne's new-found fame didn't come from his efforts during a match.It is a phenomenon that's becoming more prominent in the sports world, said Boston College professor Mike Serazio, who has researched the intersection of media and sport."You have had, in the last five to 10 years, the rise of athlete-stars who are all hype, all social media following," Serazio said. "Their fame is not commensurate to their athletic talents."Serazio said that anyone who makes it to their country's national team is highly talented. But in previous eras, athletes had to be among the very best to book a television commercial or appear on a cereal box. "You simply don't need the mass media in the way that you did previously and athletes understand this," he said. "Athletes have been taking to social media and using it ambitiously to cultivate followings, to strike brand deals, to make money, to leverage their popularity."Will fame last after the World Cup?Viral clips are the direction in which sports viewership is headed, Serazio believes."Your performance across the whole arc of the game doesn't matter as much as having a signature moment that'll play well, that'll reverberate in the viral confines of social media," Serazio said."The viral moment has greater currency," he said. "It's the thing that matters more than the game itself."The question, though, is whether a World Cup athlete who finds themselves with millions of new fans can turn that into a career beyond the white lines of the football pitch."You have a window there of attention," Sezario  said. "Nobody knew who the Cape Verde goalie was... and I don't know that they'll know who he is after the World Cup ends.""Messi, Ronaldo, Neymar, Mbappe, after they retire, they're still going to be able to do deals," he said. Sezario said there are less examples of "athletes who have one big moment being able to leverage that post career". One example of successfully harnessing a social media audience is Ilona Maher, the US rugby player whose popularity soared during the 2024 Paris Olympics. Maher has her own podcast, brand ambassador roles, modelled for Sports Illustrated, and finished as runner-up in the television series Dancing with the Stars. Maher also won the Best Breakthrough Athlete Award at the 2025 ESPY Awards.Duffy said there are long-term career opportunities available for new social media stars, but exactly how much money can be earned is hard to calculate.The price paid for sponsored posts on social media is not as standardised as rates in traditional media, such as television commercials, Duffy said. "There are very few safeguards on what a sort of reasonable income looks like," Duffy added."These are individuals whose careers so far have been hitched to soccer. So thinking about how they navigate the variability of a kind of shadowy ecosystem of the digital media economy is curious."The cultural currency for these viral World Cup stars is at a high point right now. What it means for their futures could depend on how well they can keep new fans engaged after the tournament ends.World Cup fans frustrated by 'confusing and expensive' tipping culture in US'This could only exist in America': What are foreign football fans finding in the US?

title: Netflix vs. Peacock: Which one deserves your money in 2026?
description: Netflix has award-winning originals and a smart algorithm, but Peacock counters with live sports and a lower price.

title: రెండేళ్లలో రూ.20 లక్షల కోట్లు ఆకర్షించిన ఏపీ: టీజీ భరత్
description: TG Bharat: ఆంధ్రప్రదేశ్‌ను దేశంలోనే పారిశ్రామిక పెట్టుబడులకు కేరాఫ్ అడ్రస్‌గా మార్చేందుకు రాష్ట్ర ప్రభుత్వం శరవేగంగా అడుగులు వేస్తోందని పరిశ్రమల శాఖ మంత్రి టీజీ భరత్ వెల్లడించారు....
content:
TG Bharat: ఆంధ్రప్రదేశ్‌ను దేశంలోనే పారిశ్రామిక పెట్టుబడులకు కేరాఫ్ అడ్రస్‌గా మార్చేందుకు రాష్ట్ర ప్రభుత్వం శరవేగంగా అడుగులు వేస్తోందని పరిశ్రమల శాఖ మంత్రి టీజీ భరత్ వెల్లడించారు. గత రెండు సంవత్సరాల కాలంలోనే ఏపీ ఏకంగా రూ.20 లక్షల కోట్ల మెగా పెట్టుబడులను దక్కించుకుందని ఆయన స్పష్టం చేశారు. ఇందులో రాష్ట్ర పెట్టుబడుల ప్రోత్సాహక మండలి (SIPB) ఒక్కటే రూ.11.77 లక్షల కోట్ల విలువైన ప్రాజెక్టులకు క్లియరెన్స్ ఇచ్చిందని పేర్కొన్నారు.







ఈ భారీ ప్రాజెక్టుల ద్వారా ఆంధ్రప్రదేశ్‌లోని దాదాపు 10.32 లక్షల మంది నిరుద్యోగ యువతకు సరికొత్త ఉపాధి అవకాశాలు లభించనున్నాయని మంత్రి ధీమా వ్యక్తం చేశారు. ప్రస్తుతం దేశవ్యాప్తంగా వస్తున్న మొత్తం ఇన్వెస్ట్‌మెంట్స్‌లో ఏపీ వాటానే అత్యధికంగా 24.3 శాతానికి చేరిందని, కంపెనీలకు అతి తక్కువ సమయంలో అనుమతులు ఇస్తూ రాష్ట్రం ‘స్పీడ్ ఆఫ్ డూయింగ్ బిజినెస్’ (Speed of Doing Business) విధానంలో దూసుకుపోతోందని వివరించారు.



Read also: Nara Lokesh: డిగ్రీలు కాదు.. జీవితాలను మార్చేదే నిజమైన విద్య: నారా లోకేశ్







అత్యాధునిక రంగాలపై ఫోకస్.. ‘ఒక కుటుంబం – ఒక పారిశ్రామికవేత్త’



రాష్ట్ర ఆర్థిక ముఖచిత్రాన్ని మార్చేసేలా సరికొత్త టెక్నాలజీ ఆధారిత రంగాలపై ప్రభుత్వం ప్రత్యేక శ్రద్ధ పెట్టిందని టీజీ భరత్ తెలిపారు. ఇందులో భాగంగా క్వాంటం వ్యాలీ, గ్రీన్ హైడ్రోజన్ వ్యాలీ, స్పేస్ అండ్ డ్రోన్ సిటీలతో పాటు అధునాతన స్టీల్ ప్లాంట్ల ఏర్పాటును వేగవంతం చేస్తున్నామన్నారు. యువతను కేవలం ఉద్యోగులుగానే కాకుండా సొంతంగా వ్యాపారాలు చేసేలా ప్రోత్సహించేందుకు ‘ఒక కుటుంబం – ఒక పారిశ్రామికవేత్త’ అనే వినూత్న పాలసీని అమల్లోకి తెచ్చినట్లు చెప్పారు.



TG Bharat: రతన్ టాటా హబ్, స్పేస్ టూరిజంపై ప్రత్యేక దృష్టి



యువతలోని సరికొత్త ఆవిష్కరణలను, స్టార్టప్ ఆలోచనలను ప్రోత్సహించడానికి ‘రతన్ టాటా ఇన్నోవేషన్ హబ్’ ఎంతగానో తోడ్పడుతుందని మంత్రి పేర్కొన్నారు. అలాగే, శ్రీహరికోట రాకెట్ లాంచింగ్ కేంద్రాన్ని బేస్ చేసుకుని రాష్ట్రంలో ‘స్పేస్ సిటీ’తో పాటు సరికొత్తగా ‘స్పేస్ టూరిజం’ (అంతరిక్ష పర్యాటకం)ను కూడా డెవలప్ చేస్తున్నట్లు ప్రకటించారు.



కేంద్ర ప్రభుత్వం దేశవ్యాప్తంగా రూ.500 కోట్ల ఖర్చుతో నిర్మించ తలపెట్టిన ప్రతిష్టాత్మక ‘భవ్య’ (BHAVYA) పార్కుల్లో.. కనీసం 3 నుంచి 4 పార్కులను ఆంధ్రప్రదేశ్‌కు దక్కించుకునేలా గట్టి ప్రయత్నాలు చేస్తున్నామని వివరించారు. దీనికి తోడు ఎయిర్‌స్పేస్, డిఫెన్స్ (రక్షణ రంగం) విభాగాల్లో రూ.10 వేల కోట్లకు పైగా ఇన్వెస్ట్ చేసేందుకు పలు గ్లోబల్ కంపెనీలు ఏపీతో చర్చలు జరుపుతున్నాయని మంత్రి టీజీ భరత్ స్పష్టం చేశారు.

title: Kolkata Police prohibit gatherings in front of Victoria House, deny nod for Trinamool factions’ Martyrs Day rally
USC’s Juju Watkins speaks for first time following return to court
description: Juju Watkins is back on the court for USC, and the Women of Troy should be one of the top teams in the country this year as a result
content: Entering the 2026-2027 season, there will be a ton of hype surrounding USC women’s basketball. Much of that, of course, is due to the expected return of superstar Juju Watkins. After missing the entire 2025-2026 season with a torn ACL, Watkins has resumed practicing, and will suit up for the Trojans this year.On Monday, Watkins spoke to the media for the first time since her return to the court.“It's just been a long time coming," Watkins said, via 247Sports. “I feel great. I'm really blessed to just be here, feeling back like myself and being able to practice with the team.“For me, a lot of it was just implementing myself in certain situations, and even though I wasn't playing, just continue to have my thinking cap on in terms of [my return], things I would do differently, things I would bring back to the team — just trying to kind of heighten my IQ as much as possible.“I've just been working out, grinding every day so that I could be in this position, so to see all the hard work kind of pay off right now, it's really fulfilling.”With Watkins leading the way, USC should be one of the top teams in the country this year. After four consecutive NCAA Tournament appearances, the Trojans are hoping to finally break through and make it to their first Final Four in over four decades.This article originally appeared on Trojans Wire: USC’s Juju Watkins speaks for first time following return to court

title: What grade should Packers get for 2026 offseason?
description: Most of the offseason work is done for the 32 NFL teams. What grade should the Packers get?
content: The "offseason" is essentially over for the 32 NFL teams. While the Green Bay Packers may still complete a contract extension for tight end Tucker Kraft or defensive tackle Devonte Wyatt before or during training camp, the majority of the team's work in terms of creating the coaching staff and roster is finished.What grade should the Packers get for their offseason decisions? ESPN's Seth Walder set out to grade every team's offseason, not just based on talent coming and going, but by evaluating the entirety of the offseason based on the quality of the team-building decisions. Walder gave only three teams got an "A" or an "A-", including the Miami Dolphins, who began a massive rebuild by reshaping the franchise in the Packers' image. Jon-Eric Sullivan, Jeff Hafley and Malik Willis will provide the foundation in Miami. And Walder especially liked signing Willis, who is a "risk worth taking" after proving he was ready for a bigger opportunity over two excellent seasons as Jordan Love's backup.The Packers, meanwhile, were one of five teams to receive a "B+" grade from Walder. In his analysis of the Packers offseason, Walder lauded the contract extension for Christian Watson, who he thinks has a "great chance to exceed the value of the deal" if he can stay healthy. The move Walder questioned was trading away Dontayvion Wicks, who "might have more production in him" with a bigger opportunity.Walder rightfully examined the losses of Willis, Romeo Doubs and Quay Walker through the lens of the compensatory pick process and in-house replacements, and he took a curious look at the contract signed by Rasheed Walker, who got a base value of only $4 million on a deal in Carolina despite being labeled as one of the top players in free agency. Earlier this offseason, Walder pointed to the Packers' trade of Rashan Gary -- netting a fourth-round pick from the Dallas Cowboys -- as one of the offseason's best moves. He didn't specifically mention the Gary trade in his offseason analysis, but it surely factored into Green Bay's surprisingly solid grade.The Chicago Bears also received a "B+" grade for their offseason. Chicago got great value for trading D.J. Moore and did well to reshape the team's safety position, but Walder questioned not adding at edge rusher.What grade would you give the Packers for their offseason? Green Bay stuck with coach Matt LaFleur, rebuilt spots on defense with veteran additions and opened the door to bigger opportunities for a few of the team's recent top picks, including Lukas Van Ness, Jordan Morgan and Matthew Golden. Talent exited, but for logical reasons. The Packers didn't make aggressive, headline moves, but the entire process can be viewed positively when evaluated from a macro team-building view.This article originally appeared on Packers Wire: What grade should Packers get for 2026 offseason?
